title: rubus macrostachys subsp. schlickumii
intro: Rubus macrostachys subsp. schlickumii, also known as Schlickum's raspberry or Schlickum's thimbleberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus macrostachys plant, which is a type of wild raspberry native to parts of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus macrostachys subsp. schlickumii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
